label: Mailchimp Newsletter Subscribe
summary: Newsletter subscription form that POSTs to a Next.js Route Handler and adds the email to a Mailchimp audience. Client component owns the form state + UX feedback (loading / success / already-subscribed / error); server route owns the Mailchimp REST call + email validation. Designed to degrade gracefully when MAILCHIMP_API_KEY or MAILCHIMP_AUDIENCE_ID is missing — the route returns 503 with a clear message and the form shows a 'configure newsletter to enable signups' banner so F3 build does not crash.

envVars.purpose: Mailchimp REST API key. Mailchimp → Account & Billing → Extras → API keys. Format '<hex>-us21' where suffix = data-center id. Tagged 'feature-runtime' because the route returns 503 + UI banner when missing.
ID of the audience (formerly 'list') new subscribers are added to. Find it in Mailchimp under Audience → Settings → Audience name and defaults. Same 503-fallback pattern as MAILCHIMP_API_KEY.

Optional override for the Mailchimp data-center subdomain (e.g. 'us21'). When unset, the route derives it from the API key suffix (the '-us21' part). Only set this if your key format is unusual or you proxy Mailchimp through a custom host.
